title: Lasianthus tawadae
taxonomy: Lasianthus tawadae is classified within the family Theaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the tea family. This classification is based on the plant's shared characteristics with other members of the family, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Lasianthus tawadae is further classified within the genus Lasianthus, which is a genus of shrubs and small trees native to tropical Asia. The species is also classified as an evergreen plant, meaning it retains its leaves year-round. By classifying Lasianthus tawadae within these categories, botanists can better understand the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Lasianthus tawadae, also known as Tawada lasiandra or Tawada lasianthus, is a species of plant in the family Theaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthus tawadae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lasianthus tawadae is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
